essence: Designs smoke‑control and extraction systems that keep escape routes clear and support safe evacuation during fires.

identity.title: Smoke Control Systems Designer
identity.summary: Creates layouts and specifications for smoke‑ventilation systems, including automatic vents, extraction fans, shafts, and control logic to meet fire‑safety regulations.

salary.notes: Designers specialising in high‑rise, commercial, or complex industrial smoke‑control systems can exceed £65k-£80k+.

qualifications: Training in smoke‑control system design (e.g., BS EN 12101)
Knowledge of fire dynamics, airflow modelling, and building regulations
Ability to produce compliant system drawings and specifications
Understanding of electrical systems, fans, vents, and control panels

lifestyle.description: Smoke control systems designers work on high‑rise buildings, commercial developments, and industrial facilities. They create system layouts, specify vents and fans, model airflow, and ensure compliance with strict fire‑safety standards. The role suits individuals who enjoy technical design, engineering logic, and contributing to life‑safety infrastructure.

habits: Produces accurate system drawings and airflow calculations
Follows strict design and compliance standards
Communicates clearly with installers, engineers, and building managers
Reviews architectural plans and fire strategies
Documents design decisions and performance criteria
